regime.note: STRUCTURE FIRST: this is a healthy but EXTENDED bull near all-time highs that CLOSED GREEN today (S&P +0.81%, web-verified) — SPY bull-stacked (20>50>200), −0.78% off its 52-week high, +8.84% over a rising 200-day, with 126/252-day momentum +9.2% / +22.5%. The composite regime SCORE is 0 only because Fear&Greed was unfetchable this run (VIX-only), NOT because the tape is neutral — the STRUCTURAL read is clearly bull. WHAT CHANGED TODAY (after-hours read): (1) today's long-end supply test PASSED — the $22B 30-year reopening stopped at 5.058% (vs 5.050% prior, a hair soft, NO tail) and the tape held/extended green through it into the close, so the modal 'solid auction' path played out and the immediate long-end risk is off the table; (2) VIX fell a 6th straight read to 15.84 (~−6.4% on the day) — complacency DEEPENED into the event cluster; (3) MSFT BOUNCED (380.79→384.36, mom5 -0.91→+0.02, mom21 -7.52→-6.65 = a multi-read improvement) and AI-hardware/semis firmed after the close (Lenovo/ASE/FormFactor/HPE up) — but MSFT is STILL bear-stacked below both MAs, so this is a counter-trend bounce, not a structural repair. Two things still sit on top of an unchanged frame: a FRESH, shallow leadership rotation (financials/defensives lead on 1-month RS but their 3m/6m RS are negative — tech remains the multi-month RS leader), and the one genuine structural crack, MSFT (−28.6% off its high, −12.9% below its 200-day) — still IDIOSYNCRATIC (QQQ/VGT/SPMO bull-stacked near highs). This all sits under a HAWKISH-HOLD Fed (7/28-29 re-verified ~78-85% hold / ~15-18% HIKE, no 2026 cuts): CPI 7/14 → PPI 7/15 → retail 7/16 → OPEX 7/17 → FOMC+MSFT 7/29. OVERNIGHT / FOLLOW-THROUGH posture: STAY LONG and HOLD overnight — Friday 7/10 is a quiet, catalyst-light session and there is no fresh reason to de-risk. But the tape is priced for perfection with VIX at 15.84 into next week's CPI cluster, so carry a defined-risk hedge over the weekend into 7/14; do not chase the 1-month rotation, and do not mistake MSFT's bounce for a bottom until it reclaims the 50-day (~404).

The one genuinely cheap structural asset is the thing left for dead — MSFT (−28.6% off its high) — and today's bounce is the first tell, but only a 50-day reclaim (~404) confirms mean-reversion over a dead-cat bounce.

The headline-CPI tail is EASING as crude/energy fades (WTI ~$74.6; XLE 1m RS the worst, −7.29). Now lands under a hawkish-hold Fed (7/28-29 prices ~78-85% hold / ~15-18% hike, no 2026 cuts) — so a hot print has more bite. Also the Q2 big-bank earnings kickoff week — XLF read-through.

psychology.read: REGIME (named): a calm, positive-gamma, COMPLACENT risk-on bull near all-time highs that CLOSED GREEN today (S&P +0.81%, web-verified) — SPY bull-stacked, −0.78% off its 52-week high, +8.84% over a rising 200-day, VIX 15.84 falling for a 6th read (~−6.4% today). This is STRUCTURALLY a bull; the composite score reads 0 only because Fear&Greed was unfetchable this run (VIX-only). WHAT CHANGED TODAY (after-hours): today's long-end supply test PASSED (30y stopped 5.058% vs 5.050% prior, no tail), the tape held green, VIX fell a 6th read, and MSFT BOUNCED (mom5 −0.91→+0.02, mom21 −7.52→−6.65, +0.9%) alongside an AH pop in AI-hardware/semis (Lenovo/ASE/FormFactor/HPE) — 'AI regained lost ground.' None of this changes the frame. Two features sit ON TOP of an unchanged bull: (1) a FRESH, still-shallow leadership rotation — financials/defensives (XLF/XLV/XLI/XLU) Lead on 1-month RS but their 3-/6-month RS are NEGATIVE, so tech (XLK 3m RS +19.5) remains the structural RS leader (the XLK 1m-RS drift −0.53→−0.94→−0.81→−0.82→−1.2 is a shallow, choppy wander that fails the persistence gate); and (2) one genuine structural CRACK — MSFT bear-stacked, −28.6% off its high and −12.9% below its 200-day, the AI-capex 'show-me' (software) de-rate, bouncing today but still below both MAs and so far IDIOSYNCRATIC (the AI-hardware/semis VGT/SPMO are bull-stacked near highs). CROWD POSTURE: complacent and short-vol into a KNOWN dense catalyst cluster over a weekend (CPI/PPI/retail 7/14-16 → OPEX 7/17 → FOMC+MSFT 7/29), under a re-verified HAWKISH-HOLD Fed (no 2026 cuts, a ~15-18% hike tail) that gives a hot CPI more bite; chasing the 1-month sector rotation; and only just starting to nibble at MSFT. FALSIFIERS of this read: (1) SPY losing its 50-day (~739) = first structural crack; (2) MSFT's weakness SPREADING to QQQ/VGT (idiosyncratic → systematic) = the real topping tell — OR MSFT reclaiming its 50-day (~404) = the de-rate repairing; (3) VIX inverting to backwardation (IVTS >1.0); (4) a HOT CPI (7/14) that re-arms the hike tail and breaks the OPEX level. Net posture after the close: STAY LONG and HOLD overnight into a quiet Friday, carry a CHEAP hedge OVER the weekend into the CPI cluster, trade the fresh rotation as a bounded swing (not a regime change), and do NOT treat MSFT's bounce as a confirmed bottom until it reclaims the 50-day.

psychology.tiltOnProbabilities: STRUCTURE-FIRST, anti-reactive: the fresh Tier-A MA/long-horizon slice BARELY moved run-over-run (SPY 751.02→751.71) — so the structural thesis is UNCHANGED, as it should be. The after-hours deltas (auction passed clean, VIX −6.4%, MSFT bounce, AH AI-hardware pop) are all confirmatory of the bull, none change the frame. The intraday XLK RS drift stays a do-nothing (shallow/choppy; tech is the structural leader). The financials/defensives rotation is a bounded SWING (negative 3m/6m RS); XLV firming is the marginal add. MSFT stays a top STRUCTURAL card — now a bouncing downtrend, still a downtrend until the 50-day (~404) is reclaimed. Event odds: the 30y auction RESOLVED clean (dropped from events[] as past), so the immediate long-end risk is off the table and the overnight posture is HOLD. CPI kept 28/46/26 but the hot-print asymmetry is SHARPER under the re-verified hawkish-hold Fed (a hot core re-arms the ~15-18% hike tail, not merely delays cuts). OPEX pin 0.58 (VIX low, near highs) but levels STALE 7/6 with spot now THROUGH the old call wall. MSFT miss shaded heavier for sell-the-news asymmetry in a show-me AI-capex tape. All labeled estimates seeded from FRESH Tier-A bars + web-verified calendar/auction/Fed path/WTI + STALE options (7/6) — re-seed the options/consensus live.

experimental.title: Dead-cat vs repair: counter-trend bounce durability in a structurally-broken mega-cap (idea)
experimental.note: Method: when a bear-stacked name below both its 50- and 200-day prints a MULTI-read momentum improvement (this run: MSFT mom5 −0.91→+0.02, mom21 −7.98→−7.8→−7.52→−6.65 across reads, +0.9% into the close, off-high −29.3%→−28.6%), classify it as a nascent bounce and base-rate the forward outcome — does it RECLAIM the 50-day (structural repair, tradeable long) or FAIL below it (dead-cat, the downtrend resumes)? Condition the base rate on (a) whether the name's own sector ETF is bull-stacked near highs (a supportive backdrop — this run VGT/semis are, and AI-hardware popped AH) and (b) distance to the 50-day (this run ~404, −4.9% overhead). Turns 'MSFT bounced' from a headline into a falsifiable, level-anchored call (reclaim vs reject). Inputs: brief-history.jsonl per-name mom5/mom21 runs + Tier-A maStack/ma50Dist/pctFrom52wHigh + the sector ETF's structure (all have). Directly frames this run's 'do not call the MSFT bottom until it reclaims the 50-day.' Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Mega-cap structural-breakdown vs index-at-highs divergence detector (idea)
experimental.note: Method: flag when a top-weight name is in a structural DOWNTREND (bear-stack, below its 200-day, deeply off its 52-week high) WHILE the index / its own sector ETF is bull-stacked near highs (this run: MSFT −12.9% below its 200-day, −28.6% off its high, bear-stack; QQQ/VGT bull-stacked near highs). Quantify the divergence (name 200-day distance − ETF 200-day distance, plus the 52-week-range gap) and base-rate the forward outcome: does the name mean-revert UP to the index (idiosyncratic, buy the eventual 50-day reclaim) or does the index roll over TO the name (systematic top, since the biggest weight leads the cap-weighted index)? Inputs: the Tier-A structural slice (maStack, ma200Dist, pctFrom52wHigh) — now computed per name + the ETFs. This run it remains the single most important structural read. Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Fresh-vs-entrenched rotation classifier: 1-month RS leading while 3/6-month RS lag (idea)
experimental.note: Method: classify a sector's leadership as FRESH (1-month RS positive/Leading but 3- and 6-month RS negative — this run: XLF/XLV/XLI/XLU) vs ENTRENCHED (all three horizons positive — this run: none of the tactical leaders; tech is the structural RS leader). Base-rate the forward outcome: does a fresh rotation persist (3-month RS climbs >0 = durable handoff) or fade (1-month RS rolls back = head-fake)? Turns 'rotation' from a one-number snapshot into a horizon-aware, falsifiable call. Inputs: the Tier-A rsMom1m/3m/6m per sector (now emitted). Directly frames this run's 'trade the rotation as a swing, not a regime change.' Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Persistence-gated RS-change detector (noise vs signal) (idea)
experimental.note: Method: for each momentum/RS series, compute the tail consecutive-same-direction run across the last N brief-history snapshots (the rlbrief.js consecutiveRun/isPersistentSignal helpers) and only surface a 'change' when the run is ≥ minRun OR a structural level breaks. This run is a live proof: XLK 1m RS drifted −0.53→−0.94→−0.81→−0.82→−1.2 = a shallow, choppy wander (no clean tail run) = NOISE, so 'tech rotated out' is not actionable, whereas XLF's 4.86→5.89→5.61→5.33→5.29 held a Leading regime = a real swing signal, and VIX's 6-read monotonic decline = a genuine persistent signal. Base-rate the hit-rate of persistence-gated vs raw single-slice signals to quantify how much whipsaw the gate removes. Inputs: brief-history.jsonl (have) + the pure helpers (have, selftest-covered). Promote to a visible 'signal quality' badge if recurring.

experimental.title: Complacency-into-catalyst gauge: persistent VIX decline + event density (idea)
experimental.note: Method: combine a PERSISTENT VIX decline (this run: 16.9→16.74→16.57→16.05→16.04→15.84, a 6-read fall) with a forward event-density count over the next ~10 trading days (this run: CPI/PPI/retail + OPEX + FOMC/MSFT = very dense) and the index's distance from its ATH (−0.78%) to score a 'priced-for-perfection' complacency reading — the setup where cheap protection has the best risk/reward. Base-rate forward drawdown conditioned on high-complacency-into-high-event-density. Inputs: ^VIX series + macroEvents[] + Tier-A pctFrom52wHigh (all have). Frames this run's 'carry a cheap hedge over the weekend' call. Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: VIX term-structure regime flag: contango→backwardation cross (idea)
experimental.note: Method: track IVTS = VIX/VIX3M (and VIX9D/VIX) daily; flag the cross of IVTS through 1.0 (contango→backwardation) as a regime-change trigger, with day-count of the current regime and a base-rate study of forward SPX returns / realized-vol after each cross. This run's whole calm read hinges on the curve STAYING in contango (IVTS ~0.85 carried); backwardation is the named falsifier. Inputs: ^VIX/^VIX9D/^VIX3M (add VIX9D/3M to the Tier-A fetch). Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Hawkish-hold hike-tail asymmetry gauge: how a macro print transmits when cut-odds are ~zero (idea)
experimental.note: Method: condition the market's reaction function on the VERIFIED FOMC-priced path, not just the print's surprise. This run the 7/28-29 FOMC (non-SEP) prices ~78-85% HOLD, only ~1-2% cut, and a LIVE ~15-18% HIKE tail (CME FedWatch / Kalshi, re-verified), with no 2026 cuts and a hawkish Chair. In that regime a hot CPI does NOT merely delay cuts (there are none to delay) — it RE-ARMS a hike tail, so the downside convexity of an inflation upside-surprise is larger than in a cut-path regime, even at the same surprise magnitude. Build a 'hike-tail bite' score = f(priced hike prob, priced cut prob, distance-to-ATH, VIX complacency) that scales the recommended hedge size ahead of a CPI/PPI. Base-rate forward SPX drawdown on hot-CPI prints split by whether the curve carried a live hike tail vs priced cuts. Inputs: CME FedWatch priced path (web, verify each run) + macroEvents[] + ^VIX + Tier-A pctFrom52wHigh. Directly frames this run's sharper hot-CPI asymmetry. Promote if recurring — no owning tool prices the Fed-path-conditioned reaction function today.
